issues.current_text: the dimension (1-based level) of the slice at position `idx`, increasing toward the central data array and decreasing back toward the suffix
issues.problem: The phrase 'decreasing back toward the suffix' is slightly misleading because dimension increases from prefix slices toward the center and then decreases toward suffix slices — both ends are prefix/suffix slices with dimension 1. Saying it 'decreases back toward the suffix' implies the suffix is the high end, but it is actually the low end.
issues.suggestion: the dimension (1-based level) of the slice at position `idx`: rises from 1 at the outermost prefix slice up to `count/2 + 1` at the central data array, then falls back to 1 at the outermost suffix slice

issues.current_text: the leading data array containing the leftmost elements, forwarded to the `Vector` base class
issues.problem: Mentioning that the parameter is 'forwarded to the base class' describes an implementation detail of the constructor rather than the semantic meaning of the parameter, and is not a typical Scaladoc convention. Surrounding parameter docs (e.g., `suffix1`, `length0`) describe meaning only.
issues.suggestion: the leading data array containing the leftmost elements

issues.current_text: the leading data array containing the leftmost elements, forwarded to the `VectorImpl` base class
issues.problem: Same as above — the 'forwarded to ...' clause adds implementation noise. The semantic description on its own is sufficient and consistent with the doc for `suffix1` on the same class.
issues.suggestion: the leading data array containing the leftmost elements

issues.current_text: the leading data array containing the leftmost elements, forwarded to the `BigVector` base class
issues.problem: The 'forwarded to the BigVector base class' clause is an implementation detail. Same applies to `_suffix1` and `_length0` on the same class, and to the corresponding parameters on Vector3, Vector4, Vector5, and Vector6 (lines 596-598, 733-735, 894-896, 1079-1081).
issues.suggestion: the leading data array containing the leftmost elements

issues.current_text: the single data array holding all elements of this vector (1 to `WIDTH` entries)
issues.problem: Good description, but it would be slightly clearer to note that `Vector1` is only used for non-empty small vectors (since `Vector0` handles the empty case), which is why the lower bound is 1.
issues.suggestion: the single data array holding all elements of this vector (between 1 and `WIDTH` entries, since `Vector0` handles the empty case)

issues.current_text: the underlying data array for the slice at position `idx`
issues.problem: Minor: the return type is `Array[? <: AnyRef | Null]`, which can be a multi-dimensional array (Arr1..Arr6) depending on the slice's dimension. Calling it 'the underlying data array' is accurate but does not hint at the variable nesting depth.
issues.suggestion: the underlying data array (of dimension matching the slice level) for the slice at position `idx`

general_feedback: The filled-in descriptions are accurate and follow the existing style of starting with a lowercase article and using backticks for code references. The main stylistic concern is the repeated 'forwarded to the X base class' clause on the constructor `@param` tags — this describes a constructor-forwarding implementation detail rather than the parameter's semantics, and is inconsistent with the surrounding parameter docs (`suffix1`, `length0`) which describe meaning only. The `vectorSliceDim` return description correctly captures the rise-and-fall shape but the wording 'decreasing back toward the suffix' could be clearer.
